ddls:
CREATE TABLE "code_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each code file */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the code file belongs to */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module the code file belongs to */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* ID of the programming language used in the code file */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the code file */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the code file */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the code file in bytes */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the code file was created */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the code file was last modified */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the current version of the code file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_code_files_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_code_files_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_code_files_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_code_files_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "project_description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "project_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "project_end_date" TEXT /* Expected or actual end date of the project */,
  "project_status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., active, completed, on hold) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "modules" (
  "module_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each module */,
  "module_name" TEXT /* Name of the module */,
  "module_description" TEXT /* Description of the module */,
  "module_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the module */,
  "module_end_date" TEXT /* Expected or actual end date of the module */,
  "module_status" TEXT /* Current status of the module (e.g., active, completed, on hold) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  "language_description" TEXT /* Description of the language */,
  "parser_guid_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parser GUID used for parsing the code in this language */,
  "optimization_techniques" TEXT /* Brief description of the optimization techniques used for this language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_languages_parser_guid_id FOREIGN KEY ("parser_guid_id") REFERENCES parser_guids ("guid_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parsing_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parsing result */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the code file being parsed */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the parsing result */,
  "parsetime" REAL /* Time taken to parse the code file */,
  "addtime" REAL /* Time taken to add the parsing result to the database */,
  "difftime" REAL /* Difference between parsetime and addtime */,
  "parser_version" TEXT /* Version of the parser used */,
  "parser_guid_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parser GUID used for this parsing result */,
  "parsing_errors" TEXT /* Any errors encountered during parsing */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parsing_results_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES code_files ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parsing_results_parser_guid_id FOREIGN KEY ("parser_guid_id") REFERENCES parser_guids ("guid_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "optimization_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each optimization metric */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the code file being optimized */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the optimization metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the optimization metric */,
  "metric_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the optimization metric */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the optimization metric (e.g., seconds, bytes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_optimization_metrics_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES code_files ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, team lead, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the code file being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the code file */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the code file was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device type (e.g., desktop, mobile) from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES code_files ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the code file being versioned */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "version_comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes about the version changes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES code_files ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parser_guids" (
  "guid_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parser GUID */,
  "guid_name" TEXT /* Name of the parser GUID */,
  "guid_description" TEXT /* Description of the parser GUID */,
  "parser_version" TEXT /* Version of the parser associated with the GUID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("guid_id")
)
